body: Harmless generates an additive harmonic spectrum but presents subtractive-style controls - filters and effects act on the harmonics before rendering. This gives the familiar feel of a subtractive synth while avoiding aliasing, since the maths happens on the partials rather than on a sampled waveform.

Harmonic masking lets you choose which partials are allowed to sound, carving timbre at the source. The specialised pluck filter models string decay for realistic plucked and mallet tones, and the phaser can bend pitch as well as sweep resonances for less conventional textures.
